title: uragoga abrupta
side_effects: Uragoga abrupta can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain in some individuals due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. In rare cases, long-term consumption of the plant's extracts may lead to kidney damage and liver failure. The plant's active compounds can also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, resulting in adverse reactions. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic alkaloids may also cause neurological symptoms, including headaches, dizziness, and tremors.
